justification: Both outputs handle skill accuracy and conservatism similarly, extracting well-evidenced skills without bloat. Neither output classifies projects into the required taxonomy (both use inline skill tags instead), so both score neutral on project_classification. B wins on impact_quality: for job_id 5 (Attentive AI), B explicitly cites the 52% latency reduction, 98% time savings, and FSM-driven autonomous agent with concrete metrics, while A gives a vaguer summary. B also correctly scores Kognitos (job_id 8) at 94 vs A's 90, better reflecting the near-perfect alignment with the candidate's agentic AI work, and B's reasoning for job_id 8 explicitly ties in the Cold Leads Agent FSM and end-to-end ownership with business impact. B's ranking order (8 > 1 > 5 > 2 > 6 > 7 > 3 > 4) more closely matches the expected behavior where Kognitos and Socure/Attentive AI-native roles rank highest and Cloudforce/General Dynamics rank lowest.
